info.title: Problem-Solving Process
info.description: A problem-solving process is created in reference to a specific defect. You define containment actions, identify root causes, and specify corrective and preventive measures for a problem-solving process, using either the standard 8D methodology or a custom methodology.

x-sap-shortText: Allows you to create, read, and update problem-solving processes in SAP Digital Manufacturing for Issue Resolution

regionHost.description: The data center where SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud is provisioned for your organization

tokenHost.description: The authentication server where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned for your organization

processUUID.description: Unique universal identifier of a problem-solving process

processId.description: Unique identifier of a problem-solving process

processLongDescription.description: Detailed description of a problem-solving process

methodologyId.description: Unique identifier of a problem-solving methodology. You obtain the methodologyId when you define the methodology using the Define Problem-Solving Methodologies app.

scenarioId.description:
Unique identifier of a problem-solving scenario. The scenarioID can be one of the following:
01 - Supplier Problem-Solving
02 - Customer Problem-Solving
03 - Internal Problem-Solving

processStatusId.description:
Unique identifier of processing status of a problem-solving process.
The processStatusId can be one of the following:
01 - New
02 - In Process
03 - Completed

confirmationStatusId.description:
Unique identifier of confirmation status of a problem-solving process. The confirmationStatusId can be one of the following based on the scenario you choose:
For Supplier Problem-Solving Scenario:
01 - Not Sent to Supplier
02 - Supplier’s Initial Investigation Ongoing
03 - Supplier Response Pending
04 - Problem Accepted by Supplier
05 - Problem Rejected by Supplier
06 - Solution Review Requested
07 - Solution Accepted
08 - Solution Rejected
For Customer Problem-Solving Scenario:
01 - Waiting for Initial Response
02 - Initial Investigation Ongoing
03 - Problem Rejected
04 - Problem Accepted
05 - Customer Solution Review Pending
06 - Solution Rejected by Customer
07 - Solution Accepted by Customer
For Internal Problem-Solving Scenario:
99

supplierProblemDescription.description: Problem description given by the supplier

supplierProblemLongDescription.description: Problem detailed description given by the supplier

customerProblemDescription.description: Problem description given by the customer

customerProblemLongDescription.description: Problem detailed description given by the customer

ownerOrgName.description: Organization name of the scenario host

creationMode.description: Flag identifier to determine whether a problem-solving process is a manual problem-solving process.

requestedStartDate.description: Requested start date of a problem-solving process by scenario host

requestedEndDate.description: Requested end date of a problem-solving process by scenario host

actualStartDate.description: Actual start date of a problem-solving process

actualEndDate.description: Actual end date of a problem-solving process

processLongDescription.description: Detailed description of a problem-solving process

01 for Technical
02 for Systemic

rootCauseCategory.description:
Unique identifier of a root cause category such as occurrence or non-detection, and more.

The rootCauseCategory can be one of the following:
01 - Occurrence
02 - Non-Detection
03 - 1M
04 - 2M
05 - 3M
06 - 4M
07 - 5M
08 - 6M
09 - 7M
10 - 8M

Unique identifier for a role to which any team member can belong to. For example, Team lead, Team member, and so on.
You can define team member roles using the Define Problem-Solving Roles app.

"01" for Customer's team and

"02" for Supplier's team

sendEmail.description: Flag identifier to check if the team member has email notifications enabled for a problem-solving process

"01" for Customer's team and

isContactPerson.description: Flag identifier to check if the team member is the point of contact for a problem-solving process

sendEmail.description: Flag identifier to check if the team member has email notifications enabled for a problem-solving process

count.description: The number of entities in the collection. Available when using the [$count](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_SystemQueryOptioncount) query option.

correlationId.description: Contains a correlation ID for the error.

causeMessage.description: Contains technical reason for the error.

top.name: $top
top.description: Show only the first n items, see [Paging - Top](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_SystemQueryOptiontop)

skip.name: $skip
skip.description: Skip the first n items, see [Paging - Skip](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_SystemQueryOptionskip)

search.name: $search
search.description: Search items by search phrases, see [Searching](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part1-protocol.html#sec_SystemQueryOptionsearch)

error.description: You are not authorized to call this resource
